topics.label: 統計推論
topics.blurb: 假設檢定、估計、信賴區間——被用得最多、也被誤解得最深的一塊。

en.label: Inference
en.blurb: Hypothesis tests, estimates, intervals — the most used and the most misread part of the subject.

articles.title: 為什麼叫虛無假設
articles.subtitle: 從一位喝茶的女士到一份聯署聲明：p 值爭議的觀念史
articles.summary: 「虛無」這個譯名，把 null 讀成了「什麼都沒發生」。Fisher 的 null 講的是可推翻性：實驗設下它，就是為了讓資料有東西可以反駁。這篇追一條線——品茶實驗如何生出第一個 p 值，Neyman 與 Pearson 如何把它改成一套決策規則，課本又如何把兩套邏輯焊成一個誰都沒設計過的東西，以及焊接處會裂在哪裡。

en.title: Why It Is Called the Null Hypothesis
en.subtitle: From a lady tasting tea to a signed statement: a short history of the p value quarrel
en.summary: Null was never about nothingness. Fisher's null is the hypothesis that can be nullified — set up so the data have something to argue against. This piece follows one line: how a tea party produced the first p value, how Neyman and Pearson turned it into a decision rule, how textbooks welded the two together into something neither man designed, and where that weld cracks.

glossary.oneLine: 一套會生出區間的規則。95% 是這套規則的長期命中率，不是眼前這一個區間的機率。

en.oneLine: A rule for producing intervals. The 95% is the rule's long-run hit rate, not the probability for the one interval in front of you.

glossary.oneLine: 效果有多大，用一把不隨樣本數伸縮的尺量出來。

en.oneLine: How large the effect is, on a scale that does not stretch with the sample.

glossary.oneLine: 檢定做得夠多，光靠雜訊也會冒出「顯著」。不校正，門檻就名存實亡。

en.oneLine: Run enough tests and noise alone will hand you a "significant" one. Without correction the threshold means nothing.

glossary.oneLine: 被設計來承受攻擊的那個假設：設下它，是為了讓資料有機會推翻它。

en.oneLine: The hypothesis set up to be attacked: it exists so the data have something to argue against.

glossary.oneLine: 把 Fisher 的證據邏輯和 Neyman-Pearson 的決策邏輯焊成一套固定動作，兩邊的代價都不付。

en.oneLine: Fisher's evidential logic and Neyman-Pearson's decision logic welded into a fixed routine that pays neither one's price.

glossary.oneLine: 一邊看結果一邊調分析：多收幾個樣本、換個共變量、換個結果變數，直到 p 掉到門檻底下。

en.oneLine: Adjusting the analysis while watching the result — a few more subjects, another covariate, another outcome — until p drops below the line.

glossary.oneLine: 假設虛無為真，得到「像這樣或更極端」的結果的機率。

en.oneLine: The probability of a result at least as extreme as the one you got, if the null were true.

glossary.oneLine: 把標籤重新洗牌，看看純屬巧合能做到多好，再看真實資料排在哪裡。

en.oneLine: Shuffle the labels, see how well coincidence can do, then see where the real data land.

glossary.oneLine: 在所有「顯著」的結果裡，真的有效果的佔幾成。它取決於這個領域有多少假說本來就是對的。

en.oneLine: Of everything that comes out "significant," how much is real. It depends on how many hypotheses in the field were true to begin with.

glossary.oneLine: 大量已發表的顯著結果，換一組人重做就做不出來。

en.oneLine: A large share of published significant findings do not come back when someone else runs the study again.

glossary.oneLine: 同一個統計量在反覆抽樣下會長成的樣子。p 值與信賴區間都是從它身上量出來的。

en.oneLine: The shape a statistic takes under repeated sampling. Both p values and confidence intervals are read off it.

glossary.oneLine: 事前選定的門檻：長期而言，你願意讓多少比例的真虛無被錯判為顯著。

en.oneLine: A threshold fixed in advance: the share of true nulls you are willing to reject in the long run.

glossary.oneLine: 效果真的存在時，這個研究抓得到它的機率。

en.oneLine: The probability that a study will catch an effect that is really there.

glossary.oneLine: 兩種錯法：虛無為真卻拒絕它（誤報），虛無為假卻沒拒絕（漏抓）。

en.term: Type I and Type II error
en.oneLine: Two ways to be wrong: rejecting a true null (a false alarm), or failing to reject a false one (a miss).
